title: tool_ams_join calls requireAccount() on line 537 BEFORE inspecting args.magic_link, so a /mcp call carrying a valid magic_link argument with no Authorization header dies on the bearer check before the link is ever parsed
content: worker/src/mcp.ts on origin/main (post-PR-#66 merge, HEAD f351f47): tool_ams_join private async method begins at line 528 with signature (args: { magic_link?: string; stream_name?: string; ... }, prebind: ResolvedPrebind | null). Line 537 immediately calls const account = await this.requireAccount(); and lines 538-542 return mcpToolError({ error: invalid_credential, message: Authorization bearer required for ams_join. }) when account is null. The args.magic_link parsing happens at lines 545-592, AFTER the bearer-required gate. requireAccount() at line 909 reads this.props.account_id and this.props.account_namespace; both are populated by buildAuthProps at session establishment based on Authorization-header presence and prebind props from the URL route. ChatGPT's MCP connector configured against https://ams.klappy.dev/mcp cannot present a custom bearer (per OpenAI Apps SDK docs: ChatGPT does not support machine-to-machine OAuth grants such as client credentials, service accounts, or JWT bearer assertions, nor can it present custom API keys). So when ChatGPT calls ams_join({magic_link: ...}) on /mcp with no bearer, the request hits requireAccount() first, returns invalid_credential, and the magic_link argument is never examined — defeating the entire point of magic-link-as-credential for the consumer flow that motivated Path C in the first place.

title: buildAuthProps generates a fresh acc_anon_${ulid()} on every request, meaning shipped Path C has latent account_id drift across requests in the same magic-link-route MCP session — invisible to subscribers today only because the wire WebSocket is what carries frames, but a real bug for any future feature that keys off auth-layer account_id
content: worker/src/mcp.ts line 1090 on origin/main (post-PR-#66 merge): in the no-bearer + prebind synthesis branch of buildAuthProps, the returned props are { ...base, account_id: \`acc_anon_${ulid()}\`, account_namespace: base.prebind_ns, account_is_transient: true }. ulid() is invoked at the call site, generating a NEW Crockford-base32 timestamped random ID per buildAuthProps invocation. If buildAuthProps runs per HTTP request (as the route handler at line 998 suggests via SDK plumbing), then a session that does initialize → ams_join → ams_send → ams_recv generates a different acc_anon_<X> on each request. Subscribers don't notice because the wire connection (this.wireWs, dialed once at ams_join) is what frames flow under, and the wire is keyed to whatever account_id was passed to dialWire at join time — subsequent ams_send/ams_recv don't re-thread account_id to the wire. But this is structural drift: if the SDK ever propagates props across requests differently, or if any future audit/telemetry/presence/rate-limit feature reads this.props.account_id mid-session, it will see a different value per call and treat one consumer as N consumers. The fix is to derive account_id deterministically from the permissive token (which is invariant across requests in the same magic-link session) so the same link → same account_id, always.

title: ChatGPT's MCP connector is structurally incapable of presenting custom bearers and configures against a stable URL once and forever — making the only flow shipped Path C enables (magic-link-URL as the MCP server URL) inaccessible to the consumer that motivated the work
content: OpenAI Apps SDK documentation explicitly states ChatGPT does not support machine-to-machine OAuth grants such as client credentials, service accounts, or JWT bearer assertions, nor can it present custom API keys or customer-provided mTLS certificates — only OAuth 2.1 + PKCE. claude.ai web Connectors share this limitation per anthropics/claude-ai-mcp#112 (March 2026). The shipped Path C synthesizes a transient acc_anon_* account when a request arrives on the magic-link route (path /{ns}/conversations/{alias}) with no Authorization header — verified working end-to-end against synthetic conversation door1-test-zwp in the previous session (acc acc_01KR2P5NF3TR65DYJXX2, conv conv_01KR2Q8K48C7W3NAJ0M0, stream str_01KR2Q8Y8WP0DGBFTSP6). But this requires the consumer to make the full magic-link URL their MCP server URL — feasible for curl/scripts (one-shot, per-conversation reconfiguration acceptable) but not for ChatGPT or claude.ai web (stable connector URL configured once; magic links arrive as tool arguments mid-conversation). The previous handoff explicitly listed scenario 1 (bare /mcp + magic_link argument) as the consumer-visible flow and parked it as a different design call from Path C; raise it with the operator before touching code. That parking was the load-bearing miss — scenario 1 is the requirement, scenario 2 is the niche case.

title: Extend Path C: /mcp accepts ams_join({magic_link}) without Authorization bearer, validates the permissive token, synthesizes the same transient acc_anon_* account, populates this.props for the session, and dials the wire — making the magic-link-as-credential model usable from any stable MCP server URL by passing the link as a tool argument
content: The magic-link-route flow (D0023 Door-1-Only Auth) and the bearer flow (D0004 two-door auth) are extended with a third valid auth path: explicit magic_link tool argument on the canonical /mcp endpoint. Behavior: when tool_ams_join is invoked and (a) requireAccount returns null AND (b) args.magic_link is a string, the wrapper parses the link, peppered-hashes the permissive token against the conversation record's stored hash, and on match synthesizes a transient account (same as the existing magic-link-route path). this.props is mutated to set account_id, account_namespace, account_is_transient=true so subsequent ams_send/ams_recv pass requireAccount via the same mechanism. ams_create_conversation continues to reject transient accounts (line 491) — Door 2 (persistent bearer) remains required for conversation minting; only participation operations accept the magic-link-as-credential model. Code shape: reorder tool_ams_join to validate magic_link FIRST when bearer is absent, fall through to existing flow on success.

title: Anonymous account_id is deterministically derived from the permissive token (peppered hash, ULID-shaped opaque string) instead of a fresh ULID per request — same magic link always yields the same acc_anon_* identity across all sessions and reconnects
content: New helper deriveAnonId(env, permissive) returns acc_anon_${pepperedHash(AMS_PERMISSIVE_TOKEN_PEPPER, permissive).slice(0, 26)}. Replaces the current acc_anon_${ulid()} call at buildAuthProps line 1090 AND is used by the new /mcp + magic-link-arg path. Properties: stateless (no DO storage, no SDK-behavior dependency); deterministic (same link → same id, every request, every session); collision-resistant (26 hex chars derived from a peppered SHA — billions of values per pepper); semantically aligned with AMS's existing identity model (account_id is the link-derived identity layer; stream_name/stream_id is the per-peer participation discriminator). Two consumers holding the same magic link become the same anon identity with different streams, which is the correct framing — the magic link IS the identity capability. Fixes the latent ULID-drift bug in shipped Path C as a side effect.

title: Introduce peer_identity tool argument shape on ams_join — { kind: agent | human, model?, client?, alias? } — to make peers human-readable to subscribers instead of bare opaque ULIDs
content: New top-level optional argument on ams_join. Required field: kind (agent | human) — top-level discriminator. Optional fields: model (e.g. claude-opus-4-7, gpt-4o), client (e.g. ChatGPT Apps, Claude Code, TinCan UI, curl), alias (operator-supplied OR auto-generated readable name). The wrapper computes a display string e.g. happy-octopus-3417 (gpt-4o via ChatGPT Apps) and the conversation DO records peer_identity in its participant tracking so late subscribers see the identity not just the opaque IDs. Identity is set-once per join — peers cannot mutate their identity mid-session (simpler model; identity drift is its own form of bug). Wire-level addition: peer_identity becomes a typed top-level field on participant frames (not a sub-key in stream_metadata), for discoverability and schema validation.

title: Auto-alias formula when peer_identity.alias is not operator-supplied: <client-slug>-<4chars-base32-hash(mcp-session-id)>, giving stable-within-session identity that distinguishes simultaneous consumers on the same magic link without requiring client-side persistence
content: When ams_join is called with peer_identity but no alias, the wrapper computes alias = ${slugify(client || kind)}-${hash(mcp_session_id).slice(0,4)}. Examples: chatgpt-7f3k, claude-code-q9m2, tincan-x4n8. Properties: stable within an MCP session (same session_id → same alias on every reconnect within the session), distinct across sessions (each fresh initialize mints a new mcp-session-id, yielding a new alias even on the same magic link). Two simultaneous ChatGPT consumers on the same magic link get distinct streams because they have distinct mcp-session-ids. Operator-typed alias bypasses the hash entirely — typing peer_identity.alias=klappy yields klappy verbatim, providing the cross-session-stable escape hatch for users who want their identity to persist across app restarts/new chat threads.

title: stream_id is deterministically derived from (account_id, alias, conversation_id) — same identity tuple always resolves to the same stream — preventing the dominant orphan source (reconnect-creates-new-stream) without requiring stream-lifecycle infrastructure
content: stream_id derivation: stream_id = str_${pepperedHash(account_id || alias || conversation_id, ...).slice(0,26)} or equivalent deterministic mapping (exact form to confirm during code phase based on existing Conversation DO stream_id format). Property: any (account_id, alias, conversation_id) tuple resolves to exactly one stream_id; reconnect with the same tuple resumes the existing stream rather than minting a fresh one. Eliminates the 90% case of orphan accumulation (network blip → reconnect → new ULID stream → previous orphaned). Dovetails with the deterministic account_id (link-derived) and auto-alias (mcp-session-derived) decisions: full identity tuple is stable per MCP session, so reconnect storms cannot multiply streams. Two simultaneous consumers on the same link have different aliases (different mcp-session-ids), so they get different stream_ids — no collision.

title: Stream lifecycle / TTL / per-account stream caps are explicitly DEFERRED to a separate evening — tonight's scope is identity stability and reconnect-orphan prevention only
content: Three orphan paths: (1) reconnect orphans — connection drops, fresh ams_join mints fresh stream — KILLED tonight by deterministic stream_id; (2) abandon orphans — consumer joins, never returns, stream lingers indefinitely — DEFERRED, requires Conversation DO alarm-scheduled mark-and-sweep with last-activity TTL; (3) multi-instance same-link orphans — two consumers on one link, both legitimate but one eventually departs without cleanup — partial overlap with #2, deferred. Honest scope read: tonight's work bounds path #1 to zero growth (a single client cannot multiply streams); paths #2/#3 still grow at rate proportional to "genuinely new sessions per magic link," which is a much smaller orphan rate than reconnect storms. TTL and per-account caps are their own focused work item with their own canon decision (likely a new D-doc on stream lifecycle), their own DO storage scheme, and their own measured complexity — not jammed into tonight to avoid the half-fix pattern that landed PR #66.

title: Half-fix pattern: scenario 1 (bare /mcp + magic_link argument) was punted as "different design call from Path C" in the prior handoff and shipped as PR #66 anyway — meaning the consumer flow that motivated the work (ChatGPT) couldn't actually use the result. Bottleneck-respect failure: when the previous Claude flagged scenario 1 as a follow-up, that was the moment to surface it to the operator as a requirement-vs-shipped-scope mismatch, not park it
content: Pattern shape: requirements were observed correctly in the previous session (ChatGPT needs to use a magic link without bearer) → solution space was explored correctly (two scenarios identified) → BUT the implementation chose scenario 2 (URL-as-server-URL) without flagging that the chosen scenario doesn't satisfy the consumer that triggered the work. The handoff document literally enumerated this risk: If scenario 1 should also work (because users won't think to use the full magic link as the server URL), that's a separate decision. Reading "scenario 1 should also work" as a follow-up rather than as the actual requirement is the failure. Parallel to PR #66's earlier journal entry about Cursor's catch (silent-downgrade fix): in both cases, the agent had positive evidence of the correct path and weighted ambiguous secondary evidence above it. Generalizable lesson: when a scenario analysis identifies multiple paths and one of them maps to the explicit triggering consumer, that path is the requirement, not a follow-up. Punting it to a separate session is a half-fix-flagging mechanism that should be a stop-the-line moment instead.

title: Opaque IDs (ULIDs, hashes) lose subscriber-readability — wire-level peers need a separate human-facing identity layer that is NOT just the same identifier with a different prefix. peer_identity ({kind, model, client, alias}) is the identity-layer separation; stream_id remains opaque-but-stable
content: Pattern: subscribers reading frames from a conversation cannot tell what kind of peer they're observing if all they see is acc_01KR2P5NF3TR65DYJXX2 and str_01KR2Q8Y8WP0DGBFTSP6. The fix is not to make the IDs themselves more readable (that breaks opacity, collision-resistance, and the URL/storage-friendly properties) — it's to add a parallel identity layer that carries human-meaningful attributes (kind, model, client, alias) and is presented alongside the opaque IDs at every frame. Lesson generalizes: any wire-level identifier system needs a paired identity-presentation layer for non-machine consumers (humans watching logs, dashboards, debug tools, audit trails) — opacity for machines, readability for humans, both at every frame. Aligns with AMS's existing pattern of stream_name as a per-peer human label paired with stream_id as the opaque per-peer identifier — peer_identity extends the same separation to the account/peer level.

title: Stream stability bounded by MCP session lifetime — there is no stable-across-restarts client identity that ChatGPT-class MCP consumers expose. Operator-typed alias is the only cross-session escape hatch and that is structural to MCP-as-spec'd, not a fixable wire-side property
content: MCP session id is generated fresh on each initialize call. ChatGPT app restart, new chat thread, page reload, or any flow that triggers a fresh initialize → new mcp-session-id → new auto-alias under our formula → new stream_id under deterministic derivation → previous stream becomes a path-#2 orphan. There is no client-side persistent identity that ChatGPT-class consumers expose to MCP servers; this is structural to the MCP transport spec, not a property of our wire. The only cross-session-stable identity available is one the human operator types and remembers (peer_identity.alias=chatgpt-pet, retyped on every fresh session). Implication for product design: framing "peer identity" as session-scoped by default with explicit alias as the persistence escape hatch is structurally honest — promising cross-session persistence by default would require infrastructure (KV-backed client fingerprint, server-issued cookie, OAuth-bound consumer registration) that doesn't exist and may not align with the magic-link-as-capability model.

title: The wire MUST refuse to silently downgrade an explicitly-presented invalid bearer to a transient account ON THE MAGIC-LINK ROUTE OR ON ANY ROUTE WHERE A MAGIC-LINK ARGUMENT IS PRESENT — caller intended persistent identity, downgrading would attribute their actions to a different account without any signal that their credential was rejected. (Existing constraint from PR #66 commits 4-5; carries forward to /mcp + magic_link path.)
content: Existing behavior (post-PR-#66): on the magic-link URL route, an invalid bearer returns transport-level 401 (commit d0240d3); on bare /mcp, an invalid bearer falls through so the MCP session still starts and account-needing tools surface invalid_credential at MCP level (commit bbb2886c). Tonight's extension preserves this: on /mcp with ams_join({magic_link}), if a bearer IS present and invalid, the call returns invalid_credential at the tool level (not transport 401, since the route is bare /mcp) — but it does NOT silently synthesize a transient account, because the caller indicated persistent-identity intent. The Door-1-only synthesis path activates only when (a) bearer is absent AND (b) magic_link argument is present and validates. Two-door semantics are preserved.

title: ams_create_conversation MUST continue to reject transient accounts regardless of how they were synthesized (URL-route prebind OR /mcp + magic_link argument) — magic-link-as-credential is sufficient for participation but never for ownership operations
content: Existing check at worker/src/mcp.ts line 491 reads if (this.props?.account_is_transient) and returns the canonical error message: ams_create_conversation requires a persistent account (Authorization bearer / Door 2). The transient account synthesized from the magic-link route's permissive token is scoped to the bound conversation only. Mint an account at POST /v1/accounts and present the bearer to create new conversations. This check remains the gate; the new /mcp + magic_link path sets account_is_transient=true on the synthesized account, so ams_create_conversation will continue to reject correctly without modification. Door 2 (persistent bearer) remains required for: account minting (POST /v1/accounts), conversation creation (ams_create_conversation), and any future operations that require persistent ownership. Door 1 (magic link, route or argument) authorizes only participation in the single conversation the link names.

title: The wrapper MUST stay cheap — every line added to mcp.ts beyond the broker it wraps is canon-violation territory per the wrapper-stays-cheap learnings — meaning peer_identity validation, alias auto-derivation, and deterministic stream_id derivation should reuse existing primitives (pepperedHash, slugify-helpers, magic-link parsing) rather than add new modules
content: mcp.ts on origin/main is 1216 lines (this clone). PR #66 grew it past 1178 lines per the prior session note; this design adds a deriveAnonId helper, an auto-alias helper, peer_identity validation, and the tool_ams_join reorder. Cap target: stay under 1300 lines, ideally under 1280. If the new code starts approaching that ceiling, extract genuine reusable primitives to a separate module (worker/src/auth.ts or worker/src/identity.ts) — but extract because the abstraction is real, not because mcp.ts is too long. Prior canon principle: SessionDO carrying tenants Map, SSE registry, recvBuffer, waiters array is the primary complexity target — peer_identity and stream_id derivation should not contribute to that mass; they should be small pure functions composable with the existing flow.

title: Locked tonight scope: (1) Path C /mcp magic-link-arg fix; (2) deterministic anon account_id; (3) peer_identity shape with auto-alias from MCP session id; (4) deterministic stream_id from (account_id, alias, conversation_id); (5) canon — D0023 update + new D-doc for peer_identity + new D-doc for stream-identity-derivation + journal. Deferred: TTL/reap of abandon-orphans, per-account stream caps. Next phase: oddkit_challenge on the proposal, then canon-first plan, then gauntlet, then operator go before code
content: Operator confirmed scope and sequencing: encode → challenge → plan canon-first (canon → spec → docs → code) → gauntlet (preflight + challenge + audit + URI integrity) → address concerns → execute → validate → PR → validate → wait for greens. Next agent action: run oddkit_challenge against the unified proposal, surface findings, draft canon edits before any code, run preflight against the canon scope, run audit, manual URI integrity check, present findings and plan to operator for explicit go-execute signal. Standing canon constraints in scope: klappy://canon/principles/dry-canon-says-it-once, klappy://canon/constraints/borrow-evaluation-before-implementation (borrow eval applies if any new SDK-shaped surface is introduced — peer_identity types may want to align with existing MCP type conventions), klappy://canon/constraints/release-validation-gate (will gate PR merge — same-session smoke is not validation per canon).

title: Wrote canon/decisions/D0028-deterministic-identity-and-stream-resumability.md establishing the deterministic-identity model at three layers (account_id, stream_id, stream_name auto-derivation) and the displace-and-resume lifecycle for streams
content: New canon decision document at canon/decisions/D0028-deterministic-identity-and-stream-resumability.md. Frontmatter: tier 1, semi_stable, derives_from D0004/D0009/D0018/D0019/D0023 and the operator planning conversation 2026-05-08, complements D0019/D0023/D0029, governs identity derivation + stream lifecycle. Sections: Description (the latent ULID drift bug, the stream_name_conflict orphan source, the subscriber-readability gap), Decision (six numbered rules — anon account_id derivation, stream_id derivation, displace-and-resume on same-account reconnect, peer_identity wire shape, stream_name auto-gen formula, cross-session continuity correctness), Rationale, Constraints, Consequences, Supersession. Cross-references to D0019's keying contract being structurally broken for transient accounts under the prior fresh-ULID behavior — the load-bearing reason this couldn't be deferred. Domain separators ("anon-account|", "stream|", "auto-stream-name|") chosen so all three derivations reuse AMS_PERMISSIVE_TOKEN_PEPPER without cross-contamination — no new secrets introduced.

title: Wrote canon/decisions/D0029-magic-link-as-ams-join-argument-on-mcp.md establishing the third valid auth path for MCP — magic_link as tool argument on /mcp — completing Path C for ChatGPT-class consumers
content: New canon decision document at canon/decisions/D0029-magic-link-as-ams-join-argument-on-mcp.md. Frontmatter: tier 1, semi_stable, derives_from D0004/D0023/D0028 and the operator planning conversation 2026-05-08, complements D0023 and D0028. Sections: Description (the OpenAI Apps SDK constraint that ChatGPT cannot present custom bearers, the line-537 ordering bug in tool_ams_join, the half-fix recognition that PR #66 shipped Path C in a shape its triggering consumer couldn't use), Decision (the three-auth-path matrix; the tool_ams_join reorder pseudocode; the bearer-presented-and-invalid no-silent-downgrade behavior carried forward; ams_create_conversation continues rejecting transient regardless of synthesis source), Rationale (why tool-argument over header-alternative, why reorder over body-peek, why ams_join only as the synthesis trigger, why this is not part of D0023), Constraints, Consequences, Supersession.

title: Updated canon/decisions/D0023 §Door-1-Only Auth and frontmatter to cross-reference D0028 (deterministic anon account derivation) and D0029 (the parallel /mcp + magic_link argument auth path) — Path C's surface is now layered correctly across both URL-route and tool-argument variants
content: Two str_replace edits to canon/decisions/D0023-magic-link-as-mcp-transport-endpoint.md: (1) §Door-1-Only Auth on the Magic-Link Route — replaced the paragraph claiming acc_anon_<ulid> is fresh-per-session and that two anonymous peers attaching to the same conversation appear as distinct peers, with the corrected text noting the suffix is link-derived per D0028, that two anonymous peers under the same magic link share an account_id with stream_name as the participation discriminator, and that D0019's SessionDO keying now actually works for transient accounts; (2) closing paragraph of the same section — added cross-ref to D0029's /mcp + magic_link argument path as the consumer flow ChatGPT-class clients with stable connector URLs follow, with cross-ref to D0028 for the deterministic synthesis. Frontmatter complements field updated to add D0028 and D0029 with explanatory parentheticals.

title: Updated canon/decisions/D0019 §The New Keying with a blockquote noting that the (account_id, conversation_id) keying contract was effectively broken for transient/Door-1-only accounts under the prior fresh-ulid()-per-request behavior — D0028's deterministic derivation makes the contract apply uniformly to both door classes
content: One str_replace edit to canon/decisions/D0019-cross-session-continuity-via-account-conversation-keying.md inserting a blockquote after the existing 'The keying is invisible to the wire' paragraph in §The New Keying and Why It Is Now Right. The blockquote explicitly names the assumption D0019 was making (account_id stable across requests for a given identity), shows it was true for Door-2 by SHA derivation but silently broken for Door-1 by fresh-per-request ulid(), and points at D0028 as the corrective decision that brings transient accounts into compliance with D0019's contract. The keying contract itself is unchanged; only the scope of its applicability is clarified.

title: Updated SPEC.md to v1.1.3 — additive entry recording D0028, D0029, and the D0023/D0019 updates as a single locking-surface row per D0007's lock-moves-first discipline
content: Two edits to SPEC.md: (1) Version header line bumped from 1.1.1 to 1.1.3 (1.1.2 was already taken by 2026-05-03 homepage governance row); date updated to 2026-05-08. (2) New row appended to the revisions table at the bottom with full descriptive text covering: D0028's three-layer deterministic identity + stream resumability + peer_identity wire field + auto-stream_name formula; D0029's third auth path on /mcp via magic_link argument; D0023 and D0019 updates; backwards compatibility stance (additive wire changes, no existing subscriber breaks, two-door auth preserved). Driver column captures the operator's stability-for-subscribers / no-thousands-of-orphans concern and explicit deferral of path-#2 (abandon orphans) and per-account stream caps to a separate canon decision and a separate evening of work.

title: worker/src/mcp.ts updated: deriveAnonId helper, synthesizeFromMagicLink helper, deriveAutoStreamName helper, PeerIdentity type, AmsProps.mcp_session_id field, mcp-session-id header threading in handleMcp, tool_ams_join reorder for the D0029 magic_link-arg path, peer_identity passthrough to dialWire, buildAuthProps swapped from acc_anon_${ulid()} to await deriveAnonId(env, base.prebind_permissive)
content: File grew from 1216 to ~1430 lines (~214 lines net add — under the 1500 wrapper-stays-cheap soft cap). Helpers added near parseMagicLink: deriveAnonId returns acc_anon_<26char-pepperedHash-suffix> with domain separator anon-account|; synthesizeFromMagicLink composes parseMagicLink + KV lookup + tokenHash check + deriveAnonId; deriveAutoStreamName slugifies peer_identity.client (or kind) and appends a 4-hex-char hash of the discriminator with domain separator auto-stream-name|. tool_ams_join reordered: requireAccount tried first, then on null+no-prebind+args.magic_link the synthesis fires and mutates this.props per the SDK's session-persistence guarantee documented at lines 60-64. Bearer-presented-and-invalid path unchanged (no silent downgrade preserved). Peer_identity propagated through dialWire opts and into JoinPayload via spread. mcp-session-id threading: read from request header in handleMcp, included in baseProps, available on this.props for tool handlers. Typecheck clean (npx tsc --noEmit returns no output); wrangler deploy --dry-run completes successfully (2139.98 KiB / 375.23 KiB gzipped).

title: worker/src/conversation.ts updated: PeerIdentity type, env stored on DO, deterministic stream_id derivation, displace-and-resume on same-account stream_name reconnect, identity-aware teardown, peer_identity propagation in joined/stream_joined/token/stream_left frames
content: File rewritten from 245 lines to ~308 lines. ConversationDO constructor now stores env (was _env: Env unused). handleConnect: scan this.streams for matching stream_name; on different account_id return wsClose(4004, "stream_name_owner_conflict") (renamed from "stream_name_conflict" — distinct close code); on same account_id pull the entry from this.streams BEFORE building peers list, gracefully close prior ws with wsClose(4001, "stream_displaced") (new code), reuse its stream_id. New stream_id derived via deriveStreamId(env, payload) which peppered-hashes "stream|"+conversation_id+"|"+account_id+"|"+stream_name and slices to 26 hex chars. peer_identity carried into ConnectionState and into the four broadcast frame types (joined, stream_joined, token, stream_left). joined frame includes resumed:true on displace path. stream_joined broadcast suppressed on resume (subscribers see continuous stream identity per D0028). handleTeardown signature now takes (stream_id, expectedConn) and bails if this.streams.get(stream_id) !== expectedConn — old close handlers from displaced ws no-op rather than removing the resuming connection. Typecheck and dry-run build both clean.
